title: Ray Summit 2026 | Hosted by Anyscale
gist: Landing/registration page for Ray Summit 2026 (Aug 24-26, San Francisco, hosted by Anyscale). No article body — the only extractable signal is the event's thematic focus (foundation-model training, multimodal data, physical AI, LLM reinforcement learning), its named speakers, and its audience-scale and pricing framing.

insights.claim: Anyscale is positioning Ray Summit 2026 around production-scale AI infrastructure — foundation model training, multimodal data curation, physical AI/robotics, and LLM reinforcement learning — explicitly distancing it from beginner tutorials.
insights.speaker: Anyscale / Ray
insights.so_what: Signals where the Ray/Anyscale ecosystem sees commercial demand concentrating in distributed AI infra; watch as a read on enterprise AI-infra spend themes.
insights.quote: This is not a weekend hackathon workshop or a “deploy your first model” tutorial.
insights.locator: Ray Summit 2026 Training is designed for engineers

insights.claim: Ray Summit 2026 training sessions cover distributed foundation-model training, production distributed inference via Ray Serve, scaling physical AI/robotics, real-time search/recommendation for AI commerce, and LLM post-training/high-performance serving.
insights.speaker: Anyscale / Ray
insights.so_what: The session lineup is a proxy for the workloads Ray is being deployed against in production; physical AI and AI-commerce recommendation are notable expansions beyond pure LLM training.

insights.claim: Ray Summit 2026's listed speakers include Anyscale/Ray co-founders Robert Nishihara and Ion Stoica, alongside Liam Fedus (Co-founder & Co-CEO), Felix Heide (Head of AI), Andrew Beam (CTO), and Kevin Peterson (CTO).
insights.speaker: Anyscale / Ray
insights.so_what: The speaker roster maps the Ray/Anyscale orbit; Liam Fedus (ex-OpenAI) headlining is a signal of which startups are aligning with the Ray ecosystem.

insights.claim: Listed Ray Summit 2026 sponsors span Microsoft Azure, Red Hat, Snowflake, DigitalOcean, SkyPilot, Simplismart, and WoolyAI, indicating cloud and AI-infra vendor alignment around the Ray ecosystem.
insights.speaker: Anyscale / Ray
insights.so_what: Sponsor logos (inferred from image asset names) hint at which infra vendors are courting the distributed-AI developer audience; treat as soft signal only.

insights.claim: Anyscale frames the Ray Summit 2026 audience as 2,500+ ML engineers, infra leads, and AI researchers, with early-bird passes at $200 (Summit) / $250 (Summit + Training) before July 10.
insights.speaker: Anyscale / Ray
insights.so_what: Quantifies the reach of the Ray developer community — a rough gauge of Anyscale's mindshare in the distributed-AI infra market.
insights.quote: Reach 2,500+ ML engineers, infra leads, and AI researchers.
